triageFlags.title: Vehicle Age Exceeds Maximum at Start of Agreement
triageFlags.description: The vehicle's age at the start of the agreement exceeds the maximum allowed by policy.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider selecting a newer vehicle or adjusting the agreement terms to comply with policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy states that the maximum vehicle age at the start of the agreement is 8 years (96 months). The vehicle's age of 92 months at the start of the agreement is close to the limit, requiring careful consideration.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio Exceeds Maximum for Credit Tier
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the maximum allowed for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Adjust the loan amount or increase the deposit to reduce the LTV ratio.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy specifies a maximum LTV of 100% for Tier B applicants. The current LTV of 91.11% is within this limit but should be monitored closely to ensure compliance.

content.reasoning: The identified issues, particularly the vehicle's age and LTV ratio, present moderate risks that need addressing to align with underwriting policies. Adjustments to the proposal could mitigate these risks.
